replics: Nadezhda: Hello, hello, my dear, what's your name? Would you like some tea?
Artem: Oh, tea sounds tempting, but what kind of tea, gray? I prefer green, of course. My name is Artem, by the way. What's yours?
Nadezhda: I'm Nadezhda, an ordinary grandmother. I've lived here for quite a while.
Artem: I don't fully understand what influences the world. Everything is so gray and it rains nonstop.
Nadezhda: I'm also feeling down. I have a neighbor named Matvey, a very caring man who always helped me around the house. But one time, I must have said something wrong or was just in a bad mood.
Nadezhda: He told me to back off with my advice. I know he doesn't mean any harm, but I feel strange, like no one needs me. I want to see him and feed him some pies, to do him some good.
